blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I don't know when I'll tell him.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: What if the time doesn't come naturally? What if it keeps feeling like there's a better moment ahead, until suddenly there isn't?
Lena was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Then you will have still chosen to be present in every moment before that. And that will have meant something. To both of you.
The arcade hummed. The racing game exploded in the corner as one of the kids claimed victory. The machines did what they always did.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I don't want to hurt him.
They sat in the quiet of that for a moment.
Then Lena reached under the counter and produced two tokens.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I've been banned from four machines in this building.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: And yet somehow you keep coming back.
